context.meal: buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing

episode_text:
day=18; meal=buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing; setting=Personal; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=1 min; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=no cue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=no cue; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is a cold-served item such as fruit or dessert AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected no microwave for cold meals across personal, social, TV, and work contexts, preserving intended serving temperature. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree without a strong time-pressure cue THEN 2 min BECAUSE this remains the best-supported personal default for standard warm meals. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state suggests efficiency or the meal is a warm finger-food/snack-style item with dip available THEN 1 min BECAUSE the new personal morning finger-food meal used lighter reheating, indicating some personal warm meals are preferred slightly less hot and faster to serve. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN microwave preference is mixed between 2 min and 1 min, with 2 min still the broader social-right summary and 1 min appearing for a spicy finger-food meal with ranch BECAUSE some warm social-right meals are preferred slightly less hot. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 2 min BECAUSE observed left-social warm meals align with standard reheating. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree or warm snack meal THEN heating preference is mixed, with both 1 min and 3 min observed, so 2 min is the most stable front-TV summary BECAUSE the user does not consistently want lighter or heavier reheating in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit/dessert meal THEN no microwave BECAUSE the right-TV strawberries meal follows the stable cold-food rule. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected standard reheating for a warm TV meal on the right. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected standard reheating in that media context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user selected lighter reheating for one left-work warm meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the hottest reheating level in that right-work meal, showing work-context temperature preference can vary by setup. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN no microwave BECAUSE the front-work fruit meal reinforces the stable cold-food rule.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person on the right OR a TV/device on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree or other non-cold meal THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to avoid blocking salient right-side social or visual targets in those contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit/dessert meal THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the right-TV strawberries meal shows the user does not always prioritize right-side visibility for lighter cold meals. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree or a cold fruit/dessert meal THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE observed left-social meals support left-side occlusion minimization when a social partner is on the left. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-occlusion minimization when the salient target was directly ahead. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the front-work fruit meal shows the same front-target visibility preference extends from TV to active device use. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not request left-side occlusion minimization in that media context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree or a cold fruit meal THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly declined occlusion-based adjustment in left-side work contexts. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE personal meals show no need for occlusion-based adjustment without a salient social or media target.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN medium BECAUSE this remains the best-supported comfortable default. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state suggests efficiency or the meal is a warm finger-food/snack-style item with dip available THEN fast BECAUSE the new personal finger-food meal used fast speed together with other efficiency-oriented choices, indicating that in personal meals the user can prioritize shorter meal time. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-social warm meals used medium speed, reinforcing a comfortable socially appropriate pace. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit/dessert meal or a typical warm entree THEN medium BECAUSE observed left-social meals used the same moderate pace, suggesting social meals favor comfort over maximum speed regardless of meal type. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a light cold item or a warm snack/entree THEN speed preference is mixed between fast and medium, with newer front-TV evidence favoring medium, so medium is the more conservative front-TV summary BECAUSE the user does not always prioritize maximum speed when watching TV ahead. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree or a cold fruit/dessert meal THEN medium BECAUSE observed right-TV meals used medium speed, indicating a stable moderate pace in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed while working, indicating that some task-focused meals prioritize efficiency. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed for fruit while working, suggesting the work-context preference for speed is moderated by meal type. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in that right-side work context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN medium BECAUSE the front-work fruit meal aligns with the lighter-meal pattern of keeping a comfortable moderate pace even during device use.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE this remains the conservative personal default, is physically feasible for the user, and is repeatedly supported in personal meals. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state suggests efficiency or lower-effort interaction OR food is a warm finger-food/snack-style item with dip available THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the new personal finger-food meal used direct delivery together with other low-effort, fast settings, showing that personal context can shift toward automation and reduced user movement. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN transfer preference is mixed between outside mouth transfer and inside mouth transfer BECAUSE one right-social warm meal used the more conservative outside-mouth style while another right-social finger-food meal used lower-effort inside-mouth delivery, so social-right context alone does not fix a single transfer style. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the observed left-social warm meal shows that social context can favor the more conservative, user-controlled transfer style. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit/dessert meal THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the earlier left-social light meal shows that in some social contexts the user prefers lower-effort direct delivery. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected direct delivery in front-TV contexts, indicating a stable preference to reduce the need to lean forward while attending to media ahead. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit/dessert meal THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the right-TV strawberries meal aligns with a lower-effort media-focused strategy. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected the less intrusive, more user-controlled style in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected inside-mouth transfer in that media context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree or a cold fruit meal THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected direct delivery while working, showing that active device engagement favors reduced user movement. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user also selected direct delivery in that right-side work context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the front-work fruit meal extends the work-focused pattern of preferring lower-effort direct delivery when attention is on a device ahead.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a moderate stopping distance that balances comfort and reachability across personal and social meals. IF transfer mode is inside mouth transfer AND any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN LED BECAUSE this remains the conservative low-burden default outside more specialized personal meals. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree with sauce or dip available THEN cue preference is mixed between speech + LED and no cue BECAUSE one personal sauced meal used explicit multimodal coordination while the newer personal finger-food meal suppressed the cue entirely, indicating that personal meals with dips can vary between stronger coordination and minimal interruption. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN cue preference is mixed between LED and no cue BECAUSE earlier right-social evidence favored a quiet visual cue while another right-social meal suppressed the cue entirely, suggesting the user wants low-interruption signaling in that context and may sometimes prefer none at all. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN LED BECAUSE the observed left-social warm meal shows the user can prefer a quiet visual-only cue in left-social settings. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit/dessert meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the earlier left-social light meal shows the user may want a more explicit multimodal cue in some social settings. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN front-TV cue preference is mixed between no cue and LED, with repeated evidence favoring reduced interruption but later evidence showing acceptance of a quiet visual cue, so LED is the best-supported conservative summary BECAUSE it preserves low disruption without fully suppressing readiness information. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit/dessert meal THEN LED BECAUSE the right-TV strawberries meal shows the user can prefer a quieter visual-only cue when attention is on a right-side screen during a light meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal cue in that right-TV warm meal, suggesting stronger notification can be preferred when attention is directed to a right-side screen. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED rather than suppressing cues. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN no cue BECAUSE the user selected no cue while working on a warm entree, indicating a preference to suppress readiness notifications during some task-focused meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected speech in the same work context for fruit, showing that cue preference can shift with meal type. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal cueing in that right-side work context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN speech BECAUSE the front-work fruit meal matches the left-work fruit pattern, suggesting that during work with a light meal the user prefers an audible cue over LED-only signaling.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE explicit manual initiation remains the most conservative personal default when no stronger meal-specific factor is present. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a warm meal with sauce or dip available THEN feeding initiation is mixed between open mouth and autocontinue BECAUSE one personal sauced meal used open-mouth initiation while the newer personal finger-food meal used autocontinue, showing that in personal dipped meals the user can prefer lower-effort initiation rather than button presses. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected open-mouth initiation for feeding in that work-and-fruit context, showing that for feeding the user may accept low-effort perceptual initiation rather than button press when the meal is light. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is neutral/unspecified AND food is a typical warm entree THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected open-mouth initiation in that right-side work context.
